commonsense evidence: 
An empty net goal happens when a team scores against their opponent who has pulled their goalie. Since there is no goalie in the net, the net is considered “empty"

commonsense evidence: 
In ice hockey, a shutout (SHO) is credited to a goaltender who successfully stops the other team from scoring during the entire game.

commonsense evidence: 
“Goals Against” are the number of goals recorded while the goalie is on the ice. Include all goals against during regulation and overtime play.

commonsense evidence: 
“Shot Against” are the number of shots recorded while the goalie is on the ice
